heading: Withholding of tax on dispositions of United States real property interests
label: 1445 Withholding of tax on dispositions of United States real property interests
notes: Amendments2017—Subsec. (e)(1). , in introductory provisions, substituted “the highest rate of tax in effect for the taxable year under section 11(b)” for “35 percent” and “multiplied by the gain” for “of the gain”.Pub. L. 115–97, § 13001(b)(3)(A)Subsec. (e)(2). , substituted “the highest rate of tax in effect for the taxable year under section 11(b) multiplied by the amount” for “35 percent of the amount”.Pub. L. 115–97, § 13001(b)(3)(B)Subsec. (e)(6). , substituted “the highest rate of tax in effect for the taxable year under section 11(b)” for “35 percent” and “multiplied by the amount” for “of the amount”.Pub. (e)(3) to (5). , substituted “15 percent” for “10 percent”.Pub. L. 114–113, § 324(a)Subsec. Prior to amendment, par. (1) related to notice of false affidavit; foreign corporations.Pub. L. 110–289, § 3024(c)(2)(A)Subsec. (d)(2). , amended par. (2) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (2) related to failure to furnish notice.Pub. L. 110–289, § 3024(c)(2)(B)Subsec. (f)(6). , added par. (6).Pub. L. 110–289, § 3024(b)2006—Subsec. (b)(8). , added par. (8).Pub. L. 109–222, § 506(b)Subsec. (e)(6), (7). , added par. (6) and redesignated former par. (6) as (7).Pub. L. 109–222, § 505(b)2003—Subsec. (e)(1). substituted “15 percent” for “20 percent”.Pub. L. 108–271997—Subsec. (e)(1). substituted “20 percent” for “28 percent” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 105–341996—Subsec. L. 104–1881993—Subsec. (e)(1), (2). substituted “35 percent” for “34 percent”.Pub. L. 103–661988—Subsec. (A) designation and adding subpar. (B).Pub. L. 99–514, § 1810(f)(2)Subsec. (d)(1)(A). , substituted “paragraph (2)” for “paragraph (2)(A)”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1810(f)(3)(B)Subsec. (d)(1)(B)(i). , amended cl. (i) generally. Prior to amendment, cl. (i) read as follows: “any transferor’s agent, the transferor is a foreign corporation or such agent has actual knowledge that such affidavit is false, or”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1810(f)(3)(A)Subsec. (e)(1). , substituted “34 percent” for “28 percent”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 311(b)(4), amended par. (1) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (1) read as follows: “A domestic partnership, the trustee of a domestic trust, or the executor of a domestic estate shall be required to deduct and withhold under subsection (a) a tax equal to 10 percent of any amount of which such partnership, trustee, or executor has custody which is—Pub. L. 99–514, § 1810(f)(4)“(A) attributable to the disposition of a United States real property interest (as defined in section 897(c), other than a disposition described in paragraph (4) or (5)), and“(B) either—“(i) includible in the distributive share of a partner of the partnership who is a foreign person,“(ii) includible in the income of a beneficiary of the trust or estate who is a foreign person, or“(iii) includible in the income of a foreign person under the provisions of section 671.”Subsec. (e)(2). , substituted “34 percent” for “28 percent”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 311(b)(4)Subsec. (e)(3). , inserted “The preceding sentence shall not apply if, as of the date of the distribution, interests in such corporation are not United States real property interests by reason of section 897(c)(1)(B).”Pub. L. 99–514, § 1810(f)(5)Subsec. (e)(4). , substituted “section 897” for “section 897(g)”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1810(f)(6)Subsec. (e)(6). , inserted “and regulations for the application of this subsection in the case of payments through 1 or more entities”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1810(f)(8)

children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this section, in the case of any disposition of a United States real property interest (as defined in section 897(c)) by a foreign person, the transferee shall be required to deduct and withhold a tax equal to 15 percent of the amount realized on the disposition.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: No person shall be required to deduct and withhold any amount under subsection (a) with respect to a disposition if paragraph (2), (3), (4), (5), or (6) applies to the transaction.

children.heading: Transferor furnishes nonforeign affidavit
children.label: 2 Transferor furnishes nonforeign affidavit
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (7), this paragraph applies to the disposition if the transferor furnishes to the transferee an affidavit by the transferor stating, under penalty of perjury, the transferor’s United States taxpayer identification number and that the transferor is not a foreign person.

children.heading: Nonpublicly traded domestic corporation furnishes affidavit that interests in corporation not United States real property interests
children.label: 3 Nonpublicly traded domestic corporation furnishes affidavit that interests in corporation not United States real property interests
A
children.text: the domestic corporation is not and has not been a United States real property holding corporation (as defined in section 897(c)(2)) during the applicable period specified in section 897(c)(1)(A)(ii), or

children.label: B
children.text: as of the date of the disposition, interests in such corporation are not United States real property interests by reason of section 897(c)(1)(B).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: This paragraph applies to the disposition if the transferee receives a qualifying statement at such time, in such manner, and subject to such terms and conditions as the Secretary may by regulations prescribe.

children.label: I
children.text: has reached agreement with the Secretary (or such agreement has been reached by the transferee) for the payment of any tax imposed by section 871(b)(1) or 882(a)(1) on any gain recognized by the transferor on the disposition of the United States real property interest, or

children.label: II
children.text: is exempt from any tax imposed by section 871(b)(1) or 882(a)(1) on any gain recognized by the transferor on the disposition of the United States real property interest, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the transferor or transferee has satisfied any transferor’s unsatisfied withholding liability or has provided adequate security to cover such liability.

children.heading: Residence where amount realized does not exceed $300,000
children.label: 5 Residence where amount realized does not exceed $300,000
A
children.text: the property is acquired by the transferee for use by him as a residence, and

children.label: B
children.text: the amount realized for the property does not exceed $300,000.

children.heading: Stock regularly traded on established securities market
children.label: 6 Stock regularly traded on established securities market
children.text: This paragraph applies if the disposition is of a share of a class of stock that is regularly traded on an established securities market.

children.label: i
children.text: the transferee or qualified substitute has actual knowledge that the affidavit referred to in such paragraph, or the statement referred to in paragraph (9)(A)(ii), is false, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the transferee or qualified substitute receives a notice (as described in subsection (d)) from a transferor’s agent, transferee’s agent, or qualified substitute that such affidavit or statement is false, or

children.label: B
children.text: if the Secretary by regulations requires the transferee or qualified substitute to furnish a copy of such affidavit or statement to the Secretary and the transferee or qualified substitute fails to furnish a copy of such affidavit or statement to the Secretary at such time and in such manner as required by such regulations.

children.heading: Applicable wash sales transactions
children.label: 8 Applicable wash sales transactions
children.text: No person shall be required to deduct and withhold any amount under subsection (a) with respect to a disposition which is treated as a disposition of a United States real property interest solely by reason of section 897(h)(5).

children.heading: Alternative procedure for furnishing nonforeign affidavit
children.label: 9 Alternative procedure for furnishing nonforeign affidavit
i
children.text: the affidavit specified in paragraph (2) is furnished to a qualified substitute, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the qualified substitute furnishes a statement to the transferee stating, under penalty of perjury, that the qualified substitute has such affidavit in his possession.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: B Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out this paragraph.

children.heading: Limitations on amount required to be withheld
children.label: c Limitations on amount required to be withheld

children.heading: Cannot exceed transferor’s maximum tax liability
children.label: 1 Cannot exceed transferor’s maximum tax liability

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The amount required to be withheld under this section with respect to any disposition shall not exceed the amount (if any) determined under subparagraph (B) as the transferor’s maximum tax liability.

children.heading: Request
children.label: B Request
children.text: At the request of the transferor or transferee, the Secretary shall determine, with respect to any disposition, the transferor’s maximum tax liability.

children.heading: Refund of excess amounts withheld
children.label: C Refund of excess amounts withheld
children.text: Subject to such terms and conditions as the Secretary may by regulations prescribe, a transferor may seek and obtain a refund of any amounts withheld under this section in excess of the transferor’s maximum tax liability.

children.heading: Authority of Secretary to prescribe reduced amount
children.label: 2 Authority of Secretary to prescribe reduced amount
children.text: At the request of the transferor or transferee, the Secretary may prescribe a reduced amount to be withheld under this section if the Secretary determines that to substitute such reduced amount will not jeopardize the collection of the tax imposed by section 871(b)(1) or 882(a)(1).

children.label: i
children.text: qualifying statements under subsection (b)(4),

children.label: ii
children.text: determinations of transferor’s maximum tax liability under paragraph (1), and

children.label: iii
children.text: reductions under paragraph (2) in the amount required to be withheld,

children.heading: Requests to be handled within 90 days
children.label: B Requests to be handled within 90 days
children.text: The Secretary shall take action with respect to any request described in subparagraph (A) within 90 days after the Secretary receives the request.

children.heading: Reduced rate of withholding for residence where amount realized does not exceed $1,000,000
children.label: 4 Reduced rate of withholding for residence where amount realized does not exceed $1,000,000
A
children.text: of property which is acquired by the transferee for use by the transferee as a residence,

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to which the amount realized for such property does not exceed $1,000,000, and

children.label: C
children.text: to which subsection (b)(5) does not apply,

children.heading: Liability of transferor’s agents, transferee’s agents, or qualified substitutes
children.label: d Liability of transferor’s agents, transferee’s agents, or qualified substitutes

children.heading: Notice of false affidavit; foreign corporations
children.label: 1 Notice of false affidavit; foreign corporations
A
children.text: the transferor furnishes the transferee or qualified substitute an affidavit described in paragraph (2) of subsection (b) or a domestic corporation furnishes the transferee an affidavit described in paragraph (3) of subsection (b), and

children.label: I
children.text: such agent has actual knowledge that such affidavit is false, or

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of an affidavit described in subsection (b)(2) furnished by a corporation, such corporation is a foreign corporation, or

children.label: ii
children.text: any transferee’s agent or qualified substitute, such agent or substitute has actual knowledge that such affidavit is false,

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If any transferor’s agent, transferee’s agent, or qualified substitute is required by paragraph (1) to furnish notice, but fails to furnish such notice at such time or times and in such manner as may be required by regulations, such agent or substitute shall have the same duty to deduct and withhold that the transferee would have had if such agent or substitute had complied with paragraph (1).

children.heading: Liability limited to amount of compensation
children.label: B Liability limited to amount of compensation
children.text: An agent’s or substitute’s liability under subparagraph (A) shall be limited to the amount of compensation the agent or substitute derives from the transaction.

children.label: A
children.text: in any negotiation with the transferee or any transferee’s agent related to the transaction, or

children.label: B
children.text: in settling the transaction.

children.label: A
children.text: in any negotiation with the transferor or any transferor’s agent related to the transaction, or

children.label: B
children.text: in settling the transaction.

children.heading: Settlement officer not treated as transferor’s agent
children.label: 5 Settlement officer not treated as transferor’s agent
A
children.text: The receipt and the disbursement of any portion of the consideration for the transaction.

children.label: B
children.text: The recording of any document in connection with the transaction.

children.heading: Special rules relating to distributions, etc., by corporations, partnerships, trusts, or estates
children.label: e Special rules relating to distributions, etc., by corporations, partnerships, trusts, or estates

children.heading: Certain domestic partnerships, trusts, and estates
children.label: 1 Certain domestic partnerships, trusts, and estates
A
children.text: is allocable to a foreign person who is a partner or beneficiary of such partnership, trust, or estate, or

children.label: B
children.text: is allocable to a portion of the trust treated as owned by a foreign person under subpart E of part I of subchapter J.

children.heading: Certain distributions by foreign corporations
children.label: 2 Certain distributions by foreign corporations
children.text: In the case of any distribution by a foreign corporation on which gain is recognized under subsection (d) or (e) of section 897, the foreign corporation shall deduct and withhold under subsection (a) a tax equal to the highest rate of tax in effect for the taxable year under section 11(b) multiplied by the amount of gain recognized on such distribution under such subsection.

children.heading: Distributions by certain domestic corporations to foreign shareholders
children.label: 3 Distributions by certain domestic corporations to foreign shareholders
children.text: If a domestic corporation which is or has been a United States real property holding corporation (as defined in section 897(c)(2)) during the applicable period specified in section 897(c)(1)(A)(ii) distributes property to a foreign person in a transaction to which section 302 or part II of subchapter C applies, such corporation shall deduct and withhold under subsection (a) a tax equal to 15 percent of the amount realized by the foreign shareholder. The preceding sentence shall not apply if, as of the date of the distribution, interests in such corporation are not United States real property interests by reason of section 897(c)(1)(B). Rules similar to the rules of the preceding provisions of this paragraph shall apply in the case of any distribution to which section 301 applies and which is not made out of the earnings and profits of such a domestic corporation.

children.heading: Taxable distributions by domestic or foreign partnerships, trusts, or estates
children.label: 4 Taxable distributions by domestic or foreign partnerships, trusts, or estates
children.text: A domestic or foreign partnership, the trustee of a domestic or foreign trust, or the executor of a domestic or foreign estate shall be required to deduct and withhold under subsection (a) a tax equal to 15 percent of the fair market value (as of the time of the taxable distribution) of any United States real property interest distributed to a partner of the partnership or a beneficiary of the trust or estate, as the case may be, who is a foreign person in a transaction which would constitute a taxable distribution under the regulations promulgated by the Secretary pursuant to section 897.

children.heading: Rules relating to dispositions of interest in partnerships, trusts, or estates
children.label: 5 Rules relating to dispositions of interest in partnerships, trusts, or estates
children.text: To the extent provided in regulations, the transferee of a partnership interest or of a beneficial interest in a trust or estate shall be required to deduct and withhold under subsection (a) a tax equal to 15 percent of the amount realized on the disposition.

children.heading: Distributions by regulated investment companies and real estate investment trusts
children.label: 6 Distributions by regulated investment companies and real estate investment trusts
children.text: If any portion of a distribution from a qualified investment entity (as defined in section 897(h)(4)) to a nonresident alien individual or a foreign corporation is treated under section 897(h)(1) as gain realized by such individual or corporation from the sale or exchange of a United States real property interest, the qualified investment entity shall deduct and withhold under subsection (a) a tax equal to the highest rate of tax in effect for the taxable year under section 11(b) (or, to the extent provided in regulations, 20 percent) multiplied by the amount so treated.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 7 Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to carry out the purposes of this subsection, including regulations providing for exceptions from provisions of this subsection and regulations for the application of this subsection in the case of payments through 1 or more entities.

children.heading: Transferor
children.label: 1 Transferor
children.text: The term “transferor” means the person disposing of the United States real property interest.

children.heading: Transferee
children.label: 2 Transferee
children.text: The term “transferee” means the person acquiring the United States real property interest.

children.label: A
children.text: a United States person, and

children.label: B
children.text: except as otherwise provided by the Secretary, an entity with respect to which section 897 does not apply by reason of subsection (l) thereof.

children.label: A
children.text: the maximum amount which the Secretary determines could be imposed as tax under section 871(b)(1) or 882(a)(1) by reason of the disposition, plus

children.label: B
children.text: the amount the Secretary determines to be the transferor’s unsatisfied withholding liability with respect to such interest.

children.heading: Transferor’s unsatisfied withholding liability
children.label: 5 Transferor’s unsatisfied withholding liability
children.text: The term “transferor’s unsatisfied withholding liability” means the withholding obligation imposed by this section on the transferor’s acquisition of the United States real property interest or on the acquisition of a predecessor interest, to the extent such obligation has not been satisfied.

children.label: A
children.text: the person (including any attorney or title company) responsible for closing the transaction, other than the transferor’s agent, and

children.label: B
children.text: the transferee’s agent.
